texto: I don't listen to the radio, cause I don't have a driver's license. But if I'm in L.A. or somewhere where we have to rent a car, I'll hear my songs. Sometimes I hear them when I'm in stores, and I'm still like a little kid in a candy shop: 'Oh my God, that's my song!' I don't know how that could ever get old.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Benny Blanco: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I don't listen to the radio, cause I don't have a driver's license. But if I'm in L.A. or somewhe…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que fe, god, song pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de song e candy.

frase_original: I don't listen to the radio, cause I don't have a driver's license. But if I'm in L.A. or somewhere where we have to rent a car, I'll hear my songs. Sometimes I hear them when I'm in stores, and I'm still like a little kid in a candy shop: 'Oh my God, that's my song!' I don't know how that could ever get old.
